```
1: # git-remote-ops
2: 
3: FOUNDATION task (ROI "Correctness blockers"). Nothing race-safe works until git can talk to a remote.
4: 
5: ## Why
6: `internal/git/git.go` (the exec wrapper, `Repo` rooted at `Dir`) has Commit/Merge/Worktree* but
7: no remote verbs. So commit-race re-verify (claim), pointer auto-advance, and tracked-tip sync are
8: all no-ops. `claim-repull-verify` and `worktree-sync-base` depend on this.
9: 
10: ## Do
11: Add ctx-aware methods to `Repo` mirroring existing style (use `r.Run`):
12: - `Fetch(ctx, remote, ref string) error` -> `fetch <remote> <ref> --prune`.
13: - `Pull(ctx, remote, branch string) error` -> `pull --ff-only <remote> <branch>` (no merge commits;
14:   the swarm coordinates by fast-forward + re-verify, not by merge-on-pull).
15: - `Push(ctx, remote, branch string) error` -> `push <remote> <branch>`.
16: - `HardReset(ctx, ref string) error` -> `reset --hard <ref>`.
17: Keep error wrapping consistent with `Run` (stderr surfaced). No swallowed errors.
18: 
19: ## Tests (git_test.go)
20: Create a temp bare repo as `origin` + two clones; assert Fetch/Pull fast-forwards, Push updates
21: origin, HardReset moves HEAD and discards local edits. Use `t.TempDir()`; CGO-free.
22: 
23: ## Caveats
24: - `Pull --ff-only` must error (not merge) on divergence; callers (claim) treat that as "lost race".
25: - Don't add go-git; stay on the system-git exec model (see package doc).

(End of file - total 25 lines)
</content>
```

```
<path>/home/spencer/git-repos/spencerharmon/infra-beehive/.worktrees/beehive-1782789602-253372/submodules/beehive/worktrees/bee-git-remote-ops/internal/git/git.go</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: // Package git is a thin exec wrapper around the git binary. Beehive shells out
2: // rather than linking go-git so submodule, gpg, and worktree behavior matches
3: // the system git exactly. All writes happen in worktrees, never the shared checkout.
4: package git
5: 
6: import (
7: 	"bytes"
8: 	"context"
9: 	"errors"
10: 	"fmt"
11: 	"os/exec"
12: 	"strings"
13: )
14: 
15: // ErrConflict is returned when a merge produces conflicts.
16: var ErrConflict = errors.New("git: merge conflict")
17: 
18: // Repo is a git working tree rooted at Dir.
19: type Repo struct{ Dir string }
20: 
21: // New returns a Repo rooted at dir.
22: func New(dir string) *Repo { return &Repo{Dir: dir} }
23: 
24: // Run executes git with args in the repo dir, returning trimmed stdout.
25: func (r *Repo) Run(ctx context.Context, args ...string) (string, error) {
26: 	var out, errb bytes.Buffer
27: 	cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "git", args...)
28: 	cmd.Dir = r.Dir
29: 	cmd.Stdout = &out
30: 	cmd.Stderr = &errb
31: 	if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil {
32: 		return strings.TrimSpace(out.String()), fmt.Errorf("git %s: %w: %s",
33: 			strings.Join(args, " "), err, strings.TrimSpace(errb.String()))
34: 	}
35: 	return strings.TrimSpace(out.String()), nil
36: }
37: 
38: // Head returns the short SHA of HEAD.
39: func (r *Repo) Head(ctx context.Context) (string, error) {
40: 	return r.Run(ctx, "rev-parse", "--short", "HEAD")
41: }
42: 
43: // CurrentBranch returns the checked-out branch name.
44: func (r *Repo) CurrentBranch(ctx context.Context) (string, error) {
45: 	return r.Run(ctx, "rev-parse", "--abbrev-ref", "HEAD")
46: }
47: 
48: // Commit stages everything and commits with msg. ErrNothing if tree clean.
49: var ErrNothing = errors.New("git: nothing to commit")
50: 
51: func (r *Repo) Commit(ctx context.Context, msg string) error {
52: 	if _, err := r.Run(ctx, "add", "-A"); err != nil {
53: 		return err
54: 	}
55: 	clean, err := r.Clean(ctx)
56: 	if err != nil {
57: 		return err
58: 	}
59: 	if clean {
60: 		return ErrNothing
61: 	}
62: 	_, err = r.Run(ctx, "commit", "-m", msg)
63: 	return err
64: }
65: 
66: // CommitPaths stages and commits only the given pathspecs, leaving any other
67: // working-tree changes untouched. Each honeybee works in its own beehive-repo
68: // worktree (its own index), so there is no cross-process lock contention; this
69: // keeps a commit scoped to exactly the files a step produced. ErrNothing if none
70: // of the paths changed.
71: func (r *Repo) CommitPaths(ctx context.Context, msg string, paths ...string) error {
72: 	if len(paths) == 0 {
73: 		return ErrNothing
74: 	}
75: 	args := append([]string{"add", "--"}, paths...)
76: 	if _, err := r.Run(ctx, args...); err != nil {
77: 		return err
78: 	}
79: 	statusArgs := append([]string{"status", "--porcelain", "--"}, paths...)
80: 	out, err := r.Run(ctx, statusArgs...)
81: 	if err != nil {
82: 		return err
83: 	}
84: 	if out == "" {
85: 		return ErrNothing
86: 	}
87: 	commitArgs := append([]string{"commit", "-m", msg, "--"}, paths...)
88: 	_, err = r.Run(ctx, commitArgs...)
89: 	return err
90: }
91: 
92: // Remote returns the name of the repo's default push remote ("origin" if
93: // present, else the first configured remote), or "" when the repo has none.
94: func (r *Repo) Remote(ctx context.Context) (string, error) {
95: 	out, err := r.Run(ctx, "remote")
96: 	if err != nil {
97: 		return "", err
98: 	}
99: 	names := strings.Fields(out)
100: 	for _, n := range names {
101: 		if n == "origin" {
102: 			return "origin", nil
103: 		}
104: 	}
105: 	if len(names) > 0 {
106: 		return names[0], nil
107: 	}
108: 	return "", nil
109: }
110: 
111: // Fetch updates remote-tracking refs for branch from remote.
112: func (r *Repo) Fetch(ctx context.Context, remote, branch string) error {
113: 	_, err := r.Run(ctx, "fetch", remote, branch)
114: 	return err
115: }
116: 
117: // Push pushes refspec to remote.
118: func (r *Repo) Push(ctx context.Context, remote, refspec string) error {
119: 	_, err := r.Run(ctx, "push", remote, refspec)
120: 	return err
121: }
122: 
123: // HardReset discards the worktree and index to ref.
124: func (r *Repo) HardReset(ctx context.Context, ref string) error {
125: 	_, err := r.Run(ctx, "reset", "--hard", ref)
126: 	return err
127: }
128: 
129: // RevParse resolves ref to a full commit SHA.
130: func (r *Repo) RevParse(ctx context.Context, ref string) (string, error) {
131: 	out, err := r.Run(ctx, "rev-parse", ref)
132: 	if err != nil {
133: 		return "", err
134: 	}
135: 	return strings.TrimSpace(out), nil
136: }
137: 
138: // Show returns the contents of path at ref (git show ref:path). An error means
139: // the path does not exist at that ref (e.g. the file was deleted).
140: func (r *Repo) Show(ctx context.Context, ref, path string) (string, error) {
141: 	return r.Run(ctx, "show", ref+":"+path)
142: }
143: 
144: // DiffPaths reports whether path changed between commits a and b.
145: func (r *Repo) DiffPaths(ctx context.Context, a, b, path string) (bool, error) {
146: 	out, err := r.Run(ctx, "diff", "--name-only", a, b, "--", path)
147: 	if err != nil {
148: 		return false, err
149: 	}
150: 	return strings.TrimSpace(out) != "", nil
151: }
152: 
153: // PublishToMain advances main to the current worktree branch's tip and pushes,
154: // the conflict-free way honeybees converge. It merges the latest main into the
155: // branch first (distinct session/plan files auto-merge), then updates main. With
156: // a remote it fetches+merges+pushes remote main; without one it updates local
157: // main (requires receive.denyCurrentBranch=updateInstead, set by `beehive init`).
158: // Concurrent publishers race only on the main ref: a non-fast-forward push is
159: // retried after re-merging the advanced main, never blocking on a write lock.
160: func (r *Repo) PublishToMain(ctx context.Context, remote string) error {
161: 	target := remote
162: 	if target == "" {
163: 		target = "."
164: 		// Pushing to the local repo's checked-out main requires updateInstead, else
165: 		// it is rejected outright. Ensure it (idempotent, repo-level, shared by all
166: 		// worktrees) so single-host (no-remote) honeybees can publish.
167: 		_, _ = r.Run(ctx, "config", "receive.denyCurrentBranch", "updateInstead")
168: 	}
169: 	for attempt := 0; attempt < 8; attempt++ {
170: 		if remote != "" {
171: 			if err := r.Fetch(ctx, remote, "main"); err != nil {
172: 				return err
173: 			}
174: 			if _, err := r.Run(ctx, "merge", "--no-edit", "FETCH_HEAD"); err != nil {
175: 				_, _ = r.Run(ctx, "merge", "--abort")
176: 				return ErrConflict
177: 			}
178: 		}
179: 		_, err := r.Run(ctx, "push", target, "HEAD:refs/heads/main")
180: 		if err == nil {
181: 			return nil
182: 		}
183: 		if !isNonFastForward(err) {
184: 			return err
185: 		}
186: 		// main advanced under us; merge it in and retry.
187: 		ref := "main"
188: 		if remote != "" {
189: 			ref = remote + "/main"
190: 		}
191: 		if _, err := r.Run(ctx, "merge", "--no-edit", ref); err != nil {
192: 			_, _ = r.Run(ctx, "merge", "--abort")
193: 			return ErrConflict
194: 		}
195: 	}
196: 	return fmt.Errorf("git: publish to main exhausted retries")
197: }
198: 
199: // Worktree is one entry from `git worktree list`.
200: type Worktree struct {
201: 	Path   string
202: 	Branch string
203: 	HEAD   string
204: }
205: 
206: // Worktrees lists the repo's worktrees (including the primary).
207: func (r *Repo) Worktrees(ctx context.Context) ([]Worktree, error) {
208: 	out, err := r.Run(ctx, "worktree", "list", "--porcelain")
209: 	if err != nil {
210: 		return nil, err
211: 	}
212: 	var wts []Worktree
213: 	var cur Worktree
214: 	flush := func() {
215: 		if cur.Path != "" {
216: 			wts = append(wts, cur)
217: 		}
218: 		cur = Worktree{}
219: 	}
220: 	for _, line := range strings.Split(out, "\n") {
221: 		switch {
222: 		case strings.HasPrefix(line, "worktree "):
223: 			flush()
224: 			cur.Path = strings.TrimPrefix(line, "worktree ")
225: 		case strings.HasPrefix(line, "HEAD "):
226: 			cur.HEAD = strings.TrimPrefix(line, "HEAD ")
227: 		case strings.HasPrefix(line, "branch "):
228: 			cur.Branch = strings.TrimPrefix(strings.TrimPrefix(line, "branch "), "refs/heads/")
229: 		}
230: 	}
231: 	flush()
232: 	return wts, nil
233: }
234: 
235: // UpdateLocalMain advances the repo's local main branch (and the primary
236: // working tree) to the current worktree branch's tip. main is checked out in the
237: // primary worktree, so this uses a push to "." with receive.denyCurrentBranch=
238: // updateInstead (ensured here, idempotent). A non-fast-forward is retried after
239: // merging the advanced local main; any other rejection (e.g. a dirty primary
240: // working tree) is returned verbatim so the caller can surface it.
241: func (r *Repo) UpdateLocalMain(ctx context.Context) error {
242: 	_, _ = r.Run(ctx, "config", "receive.denyCurrentBranch", "updateInstead")
243: 	for attempt := 0; attempt < 8; attempt++ {
244: 		_, err := r.Run(ctx, "push", ".", "HEAD:refs/heads/main")
245: 		if err == nil {
246: 			return nil
247: 		}
248: 		if !isNonFastForward(err) {
249: 			return err
250: 		}
251: 		if _, err := r.Run(ctx, "merge", "--no-edit", "main"); err != nil {
252: 			_, _ = r.Run(ctx, "merge", "--abort")
253: 			return ErrConflict
254: 		}
255: 	}
256: 	return fmt.Errorf("git: update local main exhausted retries")
257: }
258: 
259: func isNonFastForward(err error) bool {
260: 	s := err.Error()
261: 	// Only a genuine non-fast-forward is worth retrying after a re-merge. Other
262: 	// rejections (protected branch, no push permission, "refusing to update
263: 	// checked out branch") must surface, not loop — they all contain "rejected".
264: 	return strings.Contains(s, "non-fast-forward") ||
265: 		strings.Contains(s, "fetch first") ||
266: 		strings.Contains(s, "tip of your current branch is behind")
267: }
268: 
269: // Clean reports whether the working tree has no staged or unstaged changes.
270: func (r *Repo) Clean(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {
271: 	out, err := r.Run(ctx, "status", "--porcelain")
272: 	if err != nil {
273: 		return false, err
274: 	}
275: 	return out == "", nil
276: }
277: 
278: // Merge merges ref into the current branch. Returns ErrConflict on conflict.
279: func (r *Repo) Merge(ctx context.Context, ref string) error {
280: 	if _, err := r.Run(ctx, "merge", "--no-edit", ref); err != nil {
281: 		if c, _ := r.HasConflict(ctx); c {
282: 			return ErrConflict
283: 		}
284: 		return err
285: 	}
286: 	return nil
287: }
288: 
289: // HasConflict reports whether the tree has unmerged paths.
290: func (r *Repo) HasConflict(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {
291: 	out, err := r.Run(ctx, "ls-files", "-u")
292: 	if err != nil {
293: 		return false, err
294: 	}
295: 	return out != "", nil
296: }
297: 
298: // LastCommit returns the full SHA of the last commit touching path.
299: func (r *Repo) LastCommit(ctx context.Context, path string) (string, error) {
300: 	return r.Run(ctx, "log", "-1", "--format=%H", "--", path)
301: }
302: 
303: // WorktreeAdd creates a worktree at dir on a new branch off base. It is
304: // idempotent: any stale worktree at dir or pre-existing branch from a crashed
305: // run is pruned/discarded first so a honeybee can always claim a fresh tree.
306: func (r *Repo) WorktreeAdd(ctx context.Context, dir, branch, base string) error {
307: 	_, _ = r.Run(ctx, "worktree", "remove", "--force", dir)
308: 	_, _ = r.Run(ctx, "worktree", "prune")
309: 	_, _ = r.Run(ctx, "branch", "-D", branch)
310: 	_, err := r.Run(ctx, "worktree", "add", "-b", branch, dir, base)
311: 	return err
312: }
313: 
314: // WorktreeRemove removes the worktree at dir, force-discarding changes.
315: func (r *Repo) WorktreeRemove(ctx context.Context, dir string) error {
316: 	_, err := r.Run(ctx, "worktree", "remove", "--force", dir)
317: 	return err
318: }

(End of file - total 318 lines)
</content>
```

> 💭 Looking at the callers to understand how `Fetch` is being used — they're all passing `"main"` as the branch, and I need to think through whether adding `--prune` would be safe for these use cases or if it could change behavior unexpectedly.
> 
> The key insight is that when you run `git fetch <remote> <ref> --prune`, it only prunes remote-tracking refs that match the specific refspec being fetched. So with `git fetch origin main --prune`, only `refs/remotes/origin/main` would be a candidate for pruning if it no longer exists on the remote. This seems generally safe for the current callers since they're all fetching the same branch consistently.
>

```

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # links-graph-enforcement — cross-submodule dep gating + cycle guard
2: 
3: Beehive: links-graph-enforcement docs/bee-links-graph-enforcement-links-graph-enforcement.md
4: 
5: Branch `bee-links-graph-enforcement` (submodule beehive). Wires the previously
6: unused `internal/links` graph into task selection and the commit path: linked-
7: submodule dependencies now gate selection, and a PLAN.md dep-tag commit that
8: forms a wait cycle is rejected.
9: 
10: ## Why
11: `internal/links` (Edge, AddDep cycle-check, HasCycle) was only reachable from the
12: CLI and, in practice, dead: selection (`select.fromSubmodule` -> `plan.Candidates`
13: -> `plan.Selectable`) resolved only same-PLAN deps via `p.Task(d)`, so a dep on a
14: linked submodule's task was invisible and no wait-cycle check ran anywhere.
15: Honeybees that write dep tags to PLAN.md (never via the CLI's `links.AddDep`)
16: could deadlock the swarm with a cross-repo cycle and nothing would catch it.
17: 
18: ## What changed (how)
19: Qualified cross-submodule task id: `<submodule>:<taskid>`. A bare dep id is local.
20: 
21: - internal/links/links.go: exported `Cycle(edges)` (ordered cycle, reuses the
22:   existing DFS) and added `CyclicNodes(edges)` — Tarjan SCC returning every node
23:   on a cycle (SCC size > 1 or self-loop), deterministic over sorted adjacency.
24:   `AddDep`/`HasCycle`/`cycle` are unchanged.
25: - internal/select/graph.go (new): `Graph` is the combined, deterministic graph the
26:   selector owns. `LoadEdges(repo)` folds, across every submodule: PLAN.md dep tags
27:   (edges between qualified ids), SUBMODULE-LINKS.yaml `deps`, and the link
28:   adjacency (`submodules`). `Validate()` returns a cycle (for lint/guard);
29:   `InCycle(node)` flags cyclic nodes; `crossDepSatisfied(sm,dep)` requires a
30:   cross-submodule dep to be both authorized (sm links the target submodule) and
31:   DONE. Edge direction matches links: From depends on To.
32: - internal/select/select.go: `Select` loads the graph once and passes it down.
33:   New `graphGate` filters **main-tier (TODO)** candidates: drop tasks on a cycle,
34:   hold tasks whose cross-submodule prerequisite is unauthorized or not DONE.
35:   Recovery tiers (GC stale / arbitration / review) pass through untouched — they
36:   exist to unstick work, not start it.
37: - internal/plan/state.go: `Selectable` now skips deps containing ":" (cross-
38:   submodule) and defers them to the selector, so a TODO task with a cross-
39:   submodule dep reaches candidates instead of being silently unselectable. The
40:   plan layer stays links-free; local dep gating is unchanged.
41: - cmd/beehive/cmd_lint.go (new) + main.go: `beehive lint` loads the combined graph
42:   and exits non-zero with the cycle path on a wait cycle.
43: - internal/config/hook.go: the installed pre-commit guard now runs both checks —
44:   (1) ROI.md honeybee-protection (unchanged behavior) and (2) when a commit touches
45:   any PLAN.md it runs `beehive lint` and rejects a cycle. Restructured so the dep
46:   check runs for honeybee *and* frontend commits (the old script early-exited for
47:   non-honeybee). Best-effort: skipped if `beehive` is not on PATH. `InstallROIHook`
48:   keeps its name/signature.
49: 
50: ## Design choices / caveats
51: - select owns the combined graph; plan stays links-free (only a lexical ":" rule).
52: - Cross-submodule dep requires an explicit link (authorization). An unlinked
53:   qualified dep is treated as unsatisfiable and holds the task (surfaced by lint
54:   only if it also forms a cycle; pure unauthorized-but-acyclic deps just gate).
55: - Cycle exclusion uses on-cycle SCC membership. A task that merely depends
56:   (transitively) on a cycle is already held by normal dep gating (its prereq never
57:   goes DONE), so it is not separately flagged.
58: - The guard validates the working-tree graph at commit time (which reflects the
59:   staged PLAN.md edit); it shells to the `beehive` binary rather than re-embedding
60:   graph logic in shell. A server pre-receive (separate deferred task) still owns
61:   push-time ROI protection.
62: 
63: ## Tests
64: - internal/links: `TestCycleExported`, `TestCyclicNodes` (3-cycle membership,
65:   acyclic, self-loop).
66: - internal/plan: `TestSelectableDefersCrossSubmoduleDeps` (qualified dep deferred;
67:   local dep still gates).
68: - internal/select: `TestLinkedDepGatesSelection` (not-DONE linked dep holds the
69:   task), `TestLinkedDepDoneUnblocks` (DONE unblocks), `TestCrossDepRequiresLink`
70:   (unlinked dep gates even when DONE), `TestCyclicTasksNotSelected` (Validate +
71:   InCycle + nothing selected). e2e two-submodule hives.
72: - internal/config: `TestPreCommitHookGuards` (script wires both guards) and
73:   `TestPreCommitDepCycleGuardE2E` (real `git commit` rejected when stubbed lint
74:   fails, allowed when it passes; honeybee ROI.md commit still rejected).
75: - Full `go test ./...` green; `go vet ./...` clean; binaries build static
76:   (CGO_ENABLED=0, `not a dynamic executable`).
77: 
78: ## Follow-ups
79: - `beehive lint` validates the working tree, not the exact staged index; a partial
80:   stage that differs from the working tree could diverge. Acceptable for the dep-
81:   tag flow (honeybees stage the whole PLAN.md); tighten to `:0:`-index reads if a
82:   partial-stage path appears.
83: - SUBMODULE-LINKS.yaml `deps` (via `links.AddDep`) has no production writer yet; it
84:   is folded into the graph for completeness so a future writer is covered.
85: - Mirror the dep-cycle reject in the server-side pre-receive hook when
86:   roi-pre-receive-hook lands (pushes currently rely on the local pre-commit).

(End of file - total 86 lines)
</content>
```

```
<path>/home/spencer/git-repos/spencerharmon/infra-beehive/.worktrees/beehive-1782789602-253372/submodules/beehive/worktrees/bee-git-remote-ops/internal/git/git.go</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
111: // Fetch updates the remote-tracking ref for branch from remote, pruning the
112: // tracking ref if branch was deleted upstream. The explicit refspec scopes
113: // --prune to this branch only, so concurrent fetches of other refs are
114: // untouched. Callers read the advanced remote-tracking ref (e.g. origin/main)
115: // and fast-forward or re-verify; Fetch never moves the current branch.
116: func (r *Repo) Fetch(ctx context.Context, remote, branch string) error {
117: 	_, err := r.Run(ctx, "fetch", remote, branch, "--prune")
118: 	return err
119: }
120: 
121: // Pull fast-forwards the current branch to remote/branch. --ff-only forbids a
122: // merge commit: a divergent history errors instead of merging. The swarm
123: // converges by fast-forward + re-verify, so callers (e.g. claim) treat a
124: // non-fast-forward pull as a lost race rather than reconciling locally.
125: func (r *Repo) Pull(ctx context.Context, remote, branch string) error {
126: 	_, err := r.Run(ctx, "pull", "--ff-only", remote, branch)
127: 	return err
128: }
129: 
130: // Push pushes refspec to remote.
131: func (r *Repo) Push(ctx context.Context, remote, refspec string) error {
132: 	_, err := r.Run(ctx, "push", remote, refspec)
133: 	return err
134: }
135: 
136: // HardReset discards the worktree and index to ref.
137: func (r *Repo) HardReset(ctx context.Context, ref string) error {
138: 	_, err := r.Run(ctx, "reset", "--hard", ref)
139: 	return err
140: }

(Showing lines 111-140 of 331. Use offset=141 to continue.)
```

```
Author: spencer <the.spencer.harmon@gmail.com>
frontend-write-paths-cli: web submodule add/link reuse the CLI via internal/submod

The web frontend's submodule add only mkdir'd an inert dir (no `git submodule
add`, no repo/, no .gitmodules), and link appended raw `from: [to]` YAML with no
schema and no cycle check. Extract the CLI's vetted behavior into a shared
internal/submod package and call it from BOTH cmd/beehive and the web handlers so
they cannot drift.

- internal/submod: Add (real `git submodule add -b <branch> <url>
  submodules/<name>/repo` + worktrees dir, url/name validation, exists guard),
  LinkSubmodules (undirected link via the links schema), AddDep (directed,
  cycle-checked root dep edge), ValidName, and sentinel errors for status mapping.
- cmd/beehive/cmd_submodule.go: add/link delegate to submod (inline logic removed).
- internal/web/web.go: submoduleAdd -> submod.Add under a 2m timeout; submoduleLink
  -> submod.AddDep; sentinels map to 400/409/500; link commit tolerates ErrNothing.
- internal/web/templates/dashboard.html: add form gains url (required) + name/branch.

Tests: submod unit tests (tracked-submodule add over file-protocol, name
derivation/validation, duplicate, link both-files idempotent, AddDep cycle guard)
and web tests (real tracked add, bad-input 400, schema-valid link, cycle reject).
go test/vet clean; cmd/... build static (CGO_ENABLED=0).

Beehive: frontend-write-paths-cli docs/bee-frontend-write-paths-cli-frontend-write-paths-cli.md

--- 7597a89
Author: spencer <the.spencer.harmon@gmail.com>
honeybee: name beehive worktrees <submodule>-<epoch> for operator findability

The agent and session beehive worktrees were named bee-<epoch>, opaque to find
on disk. Now they are <submodule>-<epoch> and <submodule>-<epoch>-session.

The worktree is created before selection, so select once on the PRIMARY checkout
(read-only) to learn the submodule, name the worktrees from it, then re-root into
the isolated worktree. That primary selection is rebound onto the worktree repo
and seeds attempt 0, so the name always matches the actual first task; a
lost-claim reselect still picks a fresh task in the worktree. A nil primary
selection now exits before creating any worktrees.

=== local git identity ===
spencer
the.spencer.harmon@gmail.com

```

```
#!/usr/bin/env sh
# beehive ROI-protect hook (installed by CLI). ROI.md is human-owned; honeybees
# must never change it. Enforced here for local commits; a server pre-receive
# mirrors this for pushes.
[ "${BEEHIVE_HONEYBEE:-0}" = "1" ] || exit 0
if git diff --cached --name-only | grep -E '(^|/)ROI\.md$' >/dev/null; then
  echo "beehive: honeybee identity may not modify ROI.md" >&2
  exit 1
fi
exit 0

```
